articles.title: Rain relief for Karnataka now, heatwave from March 24: IMD issues fresh weather alert - newsfirstprime.com
articles.full_text:
Light rain and hailstorms bring temporary relief across north and south interior Karnataka; thunderstorms likely in Bengaluru for next 48 hours before heat rises again.
After days of intense summer heat, people across Karnataka have finally received some relief as rain cooled several regions in the last 24 hours. Both north and south interior parts of the state witnessed light showers, with some districts even recording hailstorms.
According to the Meteorological Department, rainfall activity is expected to continue for the next two days in different parts of the state. In the past 24 hours, Hukkeri in Belagavi district recorded 2 cm of rainfall, while Gundlupet in Chamarajanagar district received 1 cm. Areas like Athani and parts of Bengaluru South also reported rain accompanied by hailstones.
The sudden showers have slightly reduced temperatures, bringing a cooler atmosphere, especially in Bengaluru. However, this relief may not last long.
C.S. Patil, Director of the Bengaluru Meteorological Centre, has stated that the city is likely to witness thunderstorms with lightning over the next two days. The sky is expected to remain partly cloudy for the next 48 hours, with strong winds blowing at speeds of 30 to 40 kmph. The maximum temperature in Bengaluru is likely to be around 31°C, while the minimum may drop to 19°C.
Rain alerts have been issued for several districts. In north interior Karnataka, districts such as Bidar, Kalaburagi, Vijayapura, Bagalkot, Yadgir, Raichur and Koppal are expected to receive light to moderate rain with gusty winds.

In south interior Karnataka, rainfall with thunder is likely in districts including Shivamogga, Chikkamagaluru, Kodagu, Hassan, Davanagere, Ballari, Vijayanagara, Bengaluru Rural, Bengaluru Urban, Kolar and Ramanagara.
Meanwhile, coastal regions are expected to remain dry with very little chance of rain.
Weather officials have also issued a warning about rising temperatures after this brief rainy spell. From Sunday, only light rain is expected in Kodagu, Mysuru and Chikkamagaluru, while the rest of the state will turn dry. From Monday onwards, rainfall activity is likely to stop completely.
Starting March 24, temperatures across Karnataka are expected to rise sharply, bringing back intense summer heat. Authorities have advised people to be prepared for the heatwave conditions in the coming week.
